claims.claim: Exact finite tie-aware Best-of-N laws predict selected utility on finite object-candidate populations.

strength.threshold: max exact-law absolute error <= 0.015 and mean <= 0.006

claims.claim: In controlled object-centric scenes, high-N selection can increase object score while real utility stagnates or falls due to binding failures.
claims.evidence: figure1 and main_metrics.csv for the raw scenario

strength.threshold: raw high-N score gain >= 0.35, utility drop >= 0.15, tail identity error >= 0.75

claims.claim: Identity, hidden-property, and targeted-probe repairs improve selected utility in the controlled synthetic setting.

strength.threshold: combined raw Nmax gain >= 0.55 with win-rate >= 0.75, targeted hidden-property gain >= 0.12, stress combined mean utility >= 0.75

claims.claim: A CPU NumPy semi-learned object-centric model improves property, identity-alignment, and transition prediction over simple baselines on generated trajectories.

strength.threshold: property and identity margins >= 0.15, transition MSE <= 25% baseline, reward correlation >= 0.75

claims.claim: The method is validated on real robot systems.
The method establishes broad benchmark superiority over graph physics, latent, or diffusion world models.
claims.evidence: no broad benchmark suite is present
